title: Probability of Two Boxes Having the Same Number of Distinct Balls
hints: Notice the small constraints on colors and counts; this suggests an exponential-time approach over colors is feasible.
Use dynamic programming over colors, tracking the number of distinct colors in the first box and the total items placed so far.
For each color, iterate over how many of its balls go to the first box, updating the DP state for distinct counts and item totals.
